reforms.description: The creation of the Coordination of Science, Technology, and Innovation (STI) in the Office of the President aims to enhance the coordination of national digital strategies and increase R&D spending, with a goal of reaching 1% of GDP by 2018. Additionally, the budget of CONACYT was increased by about 20% in 2014 to support research and development initiatives.
reforms.component_name: STI Coordination and R&D Budget Increase
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D spending and enhancing coordination are expected to boost innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been put into effect and are operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The creation of a national coordination body and significant budget increases represent major structural changes.
reforms.source_quote: Creation of Coordination of STI in the Office of the President in January 2013, which has been coordinating the National Digital Strategy.

reforms.description: In January 2019, the government announced a package of reforms aimed at deepening the banking sector and increasing financial inclusion, which includes the development of a smartphone platform for cost-free electronic payments and allowing minors to open bank accounts.
reforms.package_name: Banking Sector Reforms for Financial Inclusion
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing financial inclusion and access to banking services is expected to support entrepreneurship and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been officially announced but are not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a significant overhaul of the banking sector aimed at increasing access and inclusion.
reforms.source_quote: In January 2019 the government announced a package of reforms to deepen the banking sector and stock market and increase financial inclusion.

reforms.description: Mexico's energy reform includes support for the deployment of clean energies through mechanisms that require electricity retailers to acquire clean energy certificates and allows suppliers to sell clean energy at fixed auction prices.
reforms.package_name: Energy Reform for Clean Energy Deployment
reforms.component_name: Clean Energy Certificates and Auction Prices
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Supporting clean energy initiatives is expected to foster innovation in the energy sector and contribute to sustainable development.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform measures are currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant shift towards clean energy and regulatory changes in the energy sector.
reforms.source_quote: Mexico's energy reform also provides support for the deployment of 'clean' energies, through two mechanisms.

reforms.description: Mexico has taken important steps to reduce barriers to entrepreneurship and streamline regulatory procedures, including the implementation of the General Law of Regulatory Improvement, which aims to extend reforms to the subnational level and create a comprehensive database of business administrative procedures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Streamlining regulatory procedures is expected to facilitate entrepreneurship and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been officially announced but is not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The implementation of a comprehensive regulatory framework represents a major reform in supporting entrepreneurship.
reforms.source_quote: Mexico has taken important steps to reduce barriers to entrepreneurship and streamline regulatory procedures, including by making more use of electronic platforms.

reforms.description: The 'Youths Building the Future' programme aims to strengthen human capital by providing internships for young people aged 18-29 who are not working or studying, along with a training scholarship to enhance their skills.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Providing internships and training scholarships is expected to enhance the skills of young people, contributing to a more skilled workforce.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme is currently operational and providing internships.
reforms.importance_rationale: While the programme is significant, it is not as transformative as major reforms.
reforms.source_quote: The programme 'Youths Building the Future' seeks to strengthen human capital accumulation by providing internships for 18-29 year olds not working or studying.
